$comment: Content schema for the PD migration assessment deck. ALL text fields are authored by Claude — the renderer contains zero business logic. The SE can edit any field before rendering.

meta.customer: string — display name of the company
meta.include_why_slide: boolean — set false for pure PD displacement where competitive framing isn't needed

slide1_title.customer_display: string — how the customer name appears on the title slide
slide1_title.tagline: string — one short line below the customer name, e.g. 'Migration Assessment' or 'Scoping Overview'

slide2_environment.subtitle: string — describes the analysis window, e.g. 'pdt-orbitpay · 90-day analysis window'

stats_top.label: string — e.g. 'Total Services'
stats_top.sub: string|null — small subtext, e.g. '237 licensed seats'

slide2_environment.context_bar: string — text inside the navy info bar, e.g. 'Plan: Enterprise · Priorities: P1–P5 · AIOps: 100 services · Event Orchestrations: 25'
slide2_environment.teaser: string — italic line previewing the next slide, e.g. 'The migration scope is much smaller than this headline — see next slide.'

slide3_scope.subtitle: string — e.g. 'Services with incidents in the last 90 days vs. dormant services'
slide3_scope.active_desc: string — Claude-authored sentence explaining what active means for THIS customer
slide3_scope.active_cta: string — e.g. '→ These migrate to incident.io'
slide3_scope.stale_desc: string — Claude-authored sentence framing the stale number positively
slide3_scope.stale_note: string — italic qualification, e.g. '60% stale — validate in Sprint 0 if this is a production account'
slide3_scope.summary_bar: string — the bottom reframe bar, e.g. 'Real migration scope: 268 active services across 61 teams — the other 404 are a service catalog cleanup opportunity'

slide4_teams.subtitle: string
slide4_teams.footer: string — Claude-authored recommendation, e.g. 'Recommended pilot: Release Engineering — 8 active services, no SN dependency, team lead is the champion'

rows.label: string
rows.badge: string — compact descriptor, e.g. '248 webhooks' or '✓ Not detected'
rows.description: string — Claude-authored explanation of what this means and how it gets handled
rows.risk: boolean — true = orange accent, false = green accent

slide6_sprint.subtitle: string — context for the plan, e.g. '268 active services · 61 teams · ServiceNow · 2 complex workflows · 14 automation actions'

sprints.label: string — e.g. 'Sprint 0'
sprints.milestone: string — Claude-authored milestone statement. Should be specific, not generic.

slide6_sprint.summary_parallel: string — e.g. '22–25 weeks ≈ 5–6 months (if PD contract deadline applies)'
slide6_sprint.summary_note: string|null — optional caveat, e.g. 'Timeline confirmed in Sprint 0 after shadow integrations are investigated'

easy_items.headline: string — short label
easy_items.detail: string — Claude-authored sentence explaining why it's genuinely easy for THIS customer

discussion_items.headline: string — short label
discussion_items.detail: string — Claude-authored explanation of what needs resolving and when

questions.question: string — the actual question to ask, specific to this customer's data
questions.why: string — why this question matters for THIS account, referencing specific numbers where possible

reasons.title: string
reasons.body: string — Claude-authored, specific to this customer's situation. References their data where relevant.

steps.title: string — e.g. 'Sprint 0 Kickoff  (1 week)'
bullets: string — Claude-authored, specific to this account

slide10_next_steps.cta: string — e.g. 'Book a kickoff call · incident.io/demo'
